scenario: Cobalt Bank migrates 24 BPMN process tests to Camunda 8.8 using the Camunda Spring Boot Starter, with 4 releases planned per month. The lead developer is wiring up the test harness for an invoice approval flow and needs to know which annotations and tools the documented Spring setup actually requires.
question: Which technical setup do the Camunda 8.8 best practices prescribe for Spring Boot process tests?

options.text: Use JUnit 5 in every test class, annotate the test with @CamundaSpringProcessTest to start a process engine, and run TestContainers via a local Docker installation to execute the test
Use JUnit 4 in every test class, annotate the test with @SpringBootTest to start a Zeebe broker, and run an in-memory broker so no Docker installation is required on the developer machine
Use TestNG in every test class, annotate the test with @ZeebeProcessTest to start a process engine, and rely on a remote shared cluster instead of TestContainers for the engine
Use JUnit 5 in every test class, annotate the test with @CamundaTest to start a process engine, and replace TestContainers with an embedded engine bundled with the Spring Boot Starter itself

a.text: Correct. The Technical setup using Spring section requires JUnit 5 (the @Test from org.junit.jupiter.api), the Camunda Spring Boot Starter, @CamundaSpringProcessTest to start the engine, and a local Docker installation to drive TestContainers.

b.text: Incorrect. JUnit 5 is mandatory and the prescribed annotation is @CamundaSpringProcessTest, not @SpringBootTest; the setup also expects TestContainers via local Docker, not an in-memory broker.

c.text: Incorrect. The best practice does not use TestNG or @ZeebeProcessTest in 8.8, and it does not point tests at a shared remote cluster as the standard setup.

d.text: Incorrect. The annotation is @CamundaSpringProcessTest, and the Camunda Spring Boot Starter does not replace TestContainers; Docker plus TestContainers is the documented mechanism.

explanation: The Camunda 8.8 Spring setup for process tests uses JUnit 5, the Camunda Spring Boot Starter, the @CamundaSpringProcessTest annotation to start the engine, and TestContainers driven by a local Docker installation.
